notes: Only medically significant spacecraft smoke event (Mir 1997 LiClO4 O2 canister fire, 6 crew exposed to dense smoke, respirators deployed). Total crewed spaceflight person-days 1961-2020: 19414 across 327 flights. ISS era (2000-present) has zero fire incidents. Guibaud 2022 catalogs 12 spacecraft fire/smoke incidents 1967-2000; others produced smoke/odour only without medical countermeasures. Current prior Gamma(2,500k) appropriate for modern spacecraft safety standards.

ISS-era (2000-2025) zero fire incidents. ~25 years x 6 crew x 365 = 54750 person-days. Improved spacecraft fire safety post-Mir; NASA OCHMO-TB-008 fire protection standard. This zero-event evidence supports low modern rate.
